summary:
Primaquine targets the sexual reproduction phase of malaria parasites making it effective against all species; chloroquine/hydroxychloroquine primarily affect the red blood cells but vary based on regional drug-resistance patterns. The correct answer aligns with this unique property provided only by primaquine which ensures no residual infectious potential remains after treatment completion. ```plaintext
Primaquine alone functions as a gametocide targeting the sexual replication cycle universally across all Plasmodium species, ensuring complete prevention of onward transmission. In contrast, while chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine are potent against intra-erythrocytic stages, their efficacy can be compromised by varying degrees of local resistance.
```

Primaquine alone functions as a gametocide targeting the sexual replication cycle universally across all Plasmodium species, ensuring complete prevention of onward transmission. ```. Answer choice: C
